rows.false_path: Continue into runtime-scope resolution and manifest-strategy selection.
rows.trigger_condition: Authorization or access-scope binding fails before any manifest allocation.

rows.false_path: Proceed to manifest reuse / continuation / replay selection.
rows.trigger_condition: Runtime scope grammar or prior-manifest context fails validation.
rows.true_path: Return `ERROR(...)` before manifest allocation or reuse.

rows.false_path: Continue into fresh, continuation, or replay manifest work.
rows.trigger_condition: The run matches a terminal manifest lineage whose `DecisionBundle` may be safely reused.
rows.true_path: Return the existing `DecisionBundle` instead of allocating a continuation child.

rows.terminal_outcome: Nonterminal lineage fork that governs the rest of the run.
rows.trigger_condition: Reuse strategy chooses fresh manifest, same-manifest reuse, continuation child, or replay child.
rows.true_path: Lineage refs, inheritance modes, and branch-decision audit context are frozen into manifest context.

rows.false_path: Continue with the sealed pre-start basis.
rows.terminal_outcome: Usually nonterminal, but may force review or child lineage before seal.
rows.trigger_condition: Late data appears while building or reusing the pre-seal context.
rows.true_path: Spawn child, surface review posture, or exclude late data according to policy bindings.

rows.false_path: Seal the manifest and permit later `RunStarted` claim.
rows.terminal_outcome: Blocked or review-required pre-start outcome vs nonterminal sealed success.
rows.trigger_condition: The ordered pre-seal gates resolve as block/review or allow seal.
rows.true_path: Persist pre-start terminal context or review-required posture and stop before `RunStarted`.

rows.false_path: Post-start faults use started-run failure handling and may enter `FAILED` semantics.
rows.terminal_outcome: Boundary between blocked pre-start and failed post-start posture.
rows.trigger_condition: A system fault happens before `RunStarted` or after command-side start.
rows.true_path: Pre-start faults finalize `BLOCKED` even if the manifest already sealed.

rows.false_path: Build or recover a fresh pre-seal basis under ordinary collection rules.
rows.terminal_outcome: Nonterminal lineage reuse that constrains later compute and audit expectations.
rows.trigger_condition: The request is a replay-capable lineage path with frozen post-seal basis available.
rows.true_path: Reuse the frozen post-seal basis and replay from deterministic lineage-bound artifacts.

rows.false_path: Proceed into drift, trust, amendment, and filing packet work.
rows.trigger_condition: Authority or filing-readiness validation yields non-pass posture before packet preparation.
rows.true_path: Emit filing or amendment gate posture and finalize blocked/review-required outcome early.

rows.false_path: Continue into amendment and filing stages.
rows.terminal_outcome: Completed, blocked, or review-required terminalization before filing logic.
rows.trigger_condition: Trust posture is already decisive and no later amendment or filing stage remains required.
rows.true_path: Publish live projections and return a terminal bundle from trust posture.

rows.false_path: No amendment progression occurs and filing logic remains ordinary.
rows.terminal_outcome: May terminalize blocked/review-required on amendment gate or continue to filing.
rows.trigger_condition: Runtime scope requests amendment intent, amendment submit, or neither.
rows.true_path: Intent-to-amend readiness and later submit readiness remain distinct state-machine progressions.

rows.false_path: End without transmit while retaining packet or trust posture as the latest truth.
rows.terminal_outcome: May remain pending external confirmation, complete, or block/review depending on authority outcome.
rows.trigger_condition: Runtime scope requests actual submission and the packet is approved to submit.
rows.true_path: Execute governed transmit, bounded recovery, and authority reconciliation.
